story_summary: Boxwell winters in Harrow Hollow, where the miller's boy Tobias begs to learn the clockmaker's trade, and Mirel sends word from the Brambled Inn.

entities.summary: A patient travelling clockmaker who carries a cedar case of tools.
entities.evidence_excerpt: Boxwell unlatched the cedar case by the hearth.

entities.summary: The discreet innkeeper of the Brambled Inn, who keeps a room for Boxwell.
entities.evidence_excerpt: A letter came from Mirel, sealed with candle-wax.

entities.summary: A snowbound village in a wooded hollow, fond of its old chapel clock.
entities.evidence_excerpt: Harrow Hollow lay deep under the first snow.

entities.summary: An eager boy who wants to learn clockmaking from Boxwell.
entities.evidence_excerpt: Tobias pressed his nose to the workshop glass every morning.

entities.summary: A retired clocksmith and Boxwell's own teacher, now living quietly in the hollow.
entities.evidence_excerpt: It was Old Gregor who first taught Boxwell to listen to a movement.

entities.summary: The hollow's herbalist, who trades remedies for repairs.
entities.evidence_excerpt: Wren left a jar of salve on the workbench.

claims.evidence_excerpt: Tobias begged to be taken on as an apprentice.
Old Gregor had been his master once.
Wren had never left the hollow.
Mirel's letter spoke to him as an old friend.

chunks.title: Boxwell in winter
chunks.body: Boxwell is wintering in Harrow Hollow, mending the village's clocks from a borrowed workshop by the mill.

chunks.title: Tobias the apprentice
chunks.body: Tobias, the miller's boy, has talked his way into Boxwell's workshop and is learning to clean and oil movements.

chunks.title: Continuity: The Apprentice
chunks.body: Previous story summary: Boxwell took the miller's boy Tobias as a winter apprentice in Harrow Hollow, while a letter arrived from Mirel.

open_questions: Will Tobias have the patience the trade demands?
